author: Omjasvin M D
title: Revamp of 825 Bus Shelters in Chennai: GCC Commits 30 Crores for Renovation | Chennai News - Times of India
text: Chennai: After years of delay, Greater Chennai Corporation on Friday floated tenders worth 30 crore to renovate and reinstall 825 bus shelters.The stainless steel shelters will soon make way for ones with digital advertisement spaces and a widened sitting area. Each of the new shelters will also be taller by 2ft. An official with the GCC's bus routes roads department said the contractor will maintain the shelters for a period of eight years and six months. \"The first six months are for demolition and reconstruction,\" he said. The contractor will have to pay GCC an ad concession of 2 lakh a year per shelter for ad space of 17 sqm.Major stretches such as Thiruvottiyur High Road, GST Road near Guindy, Anna Nagar 2nd Avenue, and Besant Nagar will get new shelters. The corporation will also install new shelters where CMRL is conducting work, such as Kodambakkam High Road, Uthamar Gandhi Salai, Arcot Road, and Loop Road. \"Eyeball counts on ads will decide the revenue,\" said the official. Another 800 shelters will be maintained by GCC.GCC so far has not reined in Finearts Communicators India Private Limited, Shine Outdoor, and Skyrams Outdoor Advertising, who won the tenders in 2015 to maintain 400 shelters. As a result, the corporation lost an annual revenue potential of 46.25 lakh, totalling 416 crore since 2015.\"The contractors never maintained the shelters nor did they provide concession to GCC. Money must be retrieved from them. GCC must not repeat the same corruption that happened in the past,\" said standing committee chairman (accounts) K Dhanasekaran.A corruption case pertaining to a 2015 bus shelter scam filed by Arappor Iyakkam, an anti-corruption NGO, in 2022, is still pending with DVAC. \"The previous firm set up shelters in random locations for advertising, and not where buses stopped. Now, the same tendering model is followed again by GCC without accountability for what happened in the past. If the contractor fails to perform, they must be terminated,\" said David Manohar of Arappor Iyakkam.
